What further plans the North
Coast railrod people have in mind
have not been given to city officials
who were informed of the terminal
plans, and in consequence there is
an amount of conjecture in the air
which has connected the North
Coast with every transcontinental
line aspiring for northwest bust
ness. Mayor Moore this morning
expressed himself pleased that the
company plans to expend such a
large sum of money on a terminal
site, but still opposes allowing it
to run it's tracks under the main
arch of the proposed new Monroe
street bridge. What we want
them to do" said the mayor this
morning, lis to cross the river
| north of the main arch. A string
of tracks under the arch to my
mind would mar the beauty of the
structure"


City Engineer Ralston today said
that he has no further information
on the North Coast plans than
those already made public. In re
gard to the line under the big arch
he expressed the opinion that there
is a possibility of the engineers de
vising a means whereby the arch
| would not be used.


Regarding terminal plans, Robert
e. Strahorn, president of the NORTH
Coast, yesterday stated that there
was no certainty of the company
closing Wall street He refuses t.


divulge any further plans of the


company, or whether the line will


continue east through the city. II,


says, however, that there are NJ


business relations with his line ant
the Inland Empire system, and tina
the North Coast will not use tn


proposed Graves tunnel.
